summary: Stancil argues that the dominant advantage in consumer AI isn’t marginally better models but the product wrapper and habits around them, with chatgpt.com as the default destination. He uses Google’s partial Windsurf acquisition to question whether Google bought the wrong half by prioritizing model talent over the product and UX expertise that actually drive usage.
money_quotes: OpenAI is winning because they built the first good chatbot product and because it became the default.
OpenAI’s biggest edge today might be that when people think to use a chatbot, they think to go to chatgpt.com.
They have unrivaled channels for distribution. What they’re missing is something that convinces people to use those models, as often as possible.
Because one thing that is undeniably true about Windsurf, which grew to a $100 million business in a matter of months, is that they built a good product.

Who is actually more valuable to Google: Some AI engineers that can make Gemini a little bit better, or the people who can make a thin, addictive product that pushes massive amounts of traffic to Gemini?
key_insight: In consumer AI, the UX wrapper and default habits matter more than small model gains, so buying talent without the product is likely the wrong bet.
